title: Hieracium pseudoplicatum
intro: Hieracium pseudoplicatum, also known as the lesser hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion-like hawkweed genus, characterized by its yellow dandelion-like flowers and jagged leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium pseudoplicatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hieracium pseudoplicatum are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems.
